title: fix release smoke dispatch permission
outOfScope: Release metadata, application code, and all workflows other than release.yml.

sources.reason: The published release run received HTTP 403 while dispatching strict post-release smoke with actions: read.
Regression coverage must preserve the required Actions permission and dispatch step.
Release completion requires post-release smoke verification.

riskAssessment.reason: The release workflow needs permission to dispatch the strict post-release smoke without weakening any release gate.

executionDecision.reason: The permission change is narrowly scoped and preserves strict post-release verification.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm actions: write is limited to dispatching smoke and no release gate is bypassed.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: release.yml grants the minimum Actions permission required to dispatch strict post-release smoke.
release.yml retains exact-SHA, tag, required-check, and strict smoke behavior.
tests/test_workflows.py verifies the permission and dispatch contract.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The release is created successfully, but the workflow cannot dispatch its required post-release smoke because the job only has actions: read.
constraints: Grant only the minimum permission needed for workflow dispatch.
Do not change release metadata or application code.
intent.rationale: actions: write is required by GitHub for workflow dispatch; retaining the existing strict smoke workflow preserves the publication evidence chain.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User confirmed the recommendation to fix the release flow rather than bypass its checks.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: release job dispatches strict post-release smoke
release gates remain unchanged while dispatch permission is expanded

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
